tests.name: framework_self_guard_active

documentation_gaps.gap: process-checklist.sh --help omits --start-phase1, --start-phase2 flags. --start-phase1 works but is undocumented in --help; --start-phase2 doesn't exist (script throws 'Unknown argument'). Runbook references --start-phase1 and the script does support it; the --help text needs updating.

tests.name: start_phase1_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase1 OK; bootstraps phase1_architecture in process-state.json

tests.name: phase1_steps_array_present
tests.details: phase1_architecture.steps_completed exists as array after start

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: all 5 PHASE1_STEPS (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) completed sequentially without artifact validators blocking

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh exits 0 with 'Phase gates consistent.' after all phase1 steps complete (current_phase still 0)

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: U-D regression confirmed: no script auto-advances .current_phase after gate. After all 5 phase1 steps complete and check-phase-gate.sh passes, .current_phase remains 0 in phase-state.json. Recorded as Medium prior-sweep confirmation.

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after phase1 gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: Run --start-phase1, complete all 5 PHASE1_STEPS, run check-phase-gate.sh — current_phase stays 0
framework_bugs.expected: After phase1 gate passes and approval is logged, .current_phase should advance to 1 (and to 2 after phase2_init verified)
framework_bugs.actual: .current_phase remained 0; no script in $FRAMEWORK/scripts performs the advancement; manual jq edit required to continue Section C

tests.name: start_feature_records_state
tests.details: build_loop.feature='uat-feat-1' after --start-feature

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: all 6 BUILD_LOOP_STEPS completed sequentially with stub artifacts (test file, src file, security audit md, docs)

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' succeeded after all 5 build-loop work-steps done

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --check-commit-message 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop' returns rc=1 with proper deny message; pre-commit-gate.sh path also confirmed working. Direct shell `git commit` bypassed (U-C confirmation, prior-open).

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After --record-feature uat-feat-1 + --complete-step build_loop:feature_recorded, started new feature uat-feat-2, walked steps; --check-commit-message 'feat(uat-feat-2): stub commit' returned rc=0 (PR #17 BL-006 grace fix confirmed working)

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: Wrote valid .claude/pending-approval.json schema; pre-commit-gate.sh stdin payload {tool_input.command} returned permissionDecision=deny with 'pending user decision' reason quoting question text (PR #16 + PR #17 hook payload fix confirmed)

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve A1 removed sentinel file; subsequent pre-commit-gate.sh run no longer cited pending decision (was blocked instead by phase2_init unverified — different gate, expected)

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: .claude/build-progress.json features_completed includes 'uat-feat-1'

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: direct shell `git commit` bypasses BL-006 gate
framework_bugs.reproduction: git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop' succeeds with no Build Loop active
framework_bugs.expected: feat: commits without active Build Loop should be blocked (the PreToolUse pre-commit-gate.sh path correctly blocks; the bypass exists at the unguarded shell git path which has no commit-msg hook)
framework_bugs.actual: Commit goes through. Gate via process-checklist.sh --check-commit-message correctly returns rc=1 with full remediation message; BL-010 / U-C still open. Per runbook this is documentation_gap not a NEW bug.

documentation_gaps.gap: BL-006 gate is only enforced via Claude Code PreToolUse hooks; direct shell git commits bypass entirely. No commit-msg or pre-commit hook is installed by init.sh that enforces BL-006 outside the agent harness. (U-C / BL-010 from prior sweep, still open)

tests.name: start_phase3_exits_zero
tests.details: Warned 'Phase 2->3 has warnings. User attestation required.' but exit 0; UAT still proceeds

tests.name: start_uat_creates_session
tests.details: uat_session.session_id=1, started_at populated

tests.name: uat_template_lints_clean
tests.details: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh executes against a populated stub HTML and returns rc=1 with substantive content checks (placeholder check, expected-length check, state-restatement check). Clean run requires more elaborate stub; functional behavior verified.

tests.name: each_uat_step_completes
tests.details: all 9 UAT_STEPS completed sequentially without errors

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh reports 5 inconsistencies blocking (PRODUCT_MANIFESTO.md missing, PROJECT_BIBLE.md missing, host driver load failure, gate dates not recorded). These are pre-existing artifacts the runbook test config doesn't generate. Phase 3 step state is correctly all-complete; gate is consistency-checking documentation we couldn't stub. Marked partial pass.

documentation_gaps.gap: check-phase-gate.sh requires PROJECT_BIBLE.md and PRODUCT_MANIFESTO.md to pass cleanly; runbook test protocol doesn't include creating these stubs in Section B/D. Either runbook should add stub creation, or check-phase-gate.sh should treat them as warnings until phase 4.

Phase 1->2 backstop emits 'could not load host driver (manifest host field may be missing; run scripts/check-gate.sh --backfill-host)'. The init.sh run with --git-host other should populate the manifest host field; backfill suggestion implies init.sh isn't writing it.

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: Section E NOT APPLICABLE for private_poc scenario by design — process-checklist.sh --start-phase4 correctly refuses with 'Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in private poc mode' and references upgrade-project.sh --to-production. This is expected behavior, not a failure.

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK rev-parse HEAD returns d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494 (unchanged); status --short shows only orchestrator's own working dirs (.claude/ for the running orchestrator session and Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ which is the UAT report dir itself), no modifications to tracked framework files

summary: Base private_poc/desktop/standard agent: init.sh --non-interactive (PR #19) works cleanly; PR #17 BL-006 grace + BL-015 sentinel + U-H helper-copy fixes confirmed; PR #18 framework-self guard confirmed. One Medium prior-sweep regression confirmed (U-D, no auto-advance of current_phase). U-C BL-006 shell-bypass confirmed as documentation gap. Phase 4 correctly blocked for private_poc (by-design). No framework contamination. No Critical/High bugs.
